title: Tech Sector Job Displacement by 2030
description: Projected percentage of current tech sector jobs that will be automated or significantly restructured by AI by 2030. Only data points where sources directly state a displacement figure are plotted.

overlays.label: GitHub Copilot RCT: developers 55.8% faster
WEF: 44% of workers' core skills will be disrupted in the next five years
McKinsey: GenAI could automate up to 70% of business activities across occupations
Goldman Sachs: AI could expose 300M full-time jobs globally to automation
MIT: Only 23% of vision-task wages cost-effective to automate with AI today
Acemoglu: ~5% of tasks fully automatable; job impact more modest than claimed
Gambacorta et al.: CodeFuse → 55% increase in code output at Ant Group
CS grads: 7.2% unemployed vs 4.5% overall (2.7pp gap)
Yeverechyahu et al.: GitHub Copilot → 37-55% increase in commits
Paradis et al. (Google): 21% reduction in coding time per task
Forrester: AI and automation will take 6% of US jobs by 2030
HBS: 17% decrease in postings for top-quartile automation-potential occupations
Meta: 'Year of Efficiency' cut ~21K jobs; AI-driven productivity enables smaller teams
Alphabet: AI tools reduced need for certain engineering and support roles
BLS OOH: Computer programmer employment projected to decline 9.6% (2023-2033)
Microsoft/Accenture: 26% more pull requests with Copilot
JPMorgan: Tech/info/media employment stopped growing at end of 2022
OECD: Employment Outlook 2025 on AI and the labour market
Goldman: Young tech workers (20-30) unemployment risen ~3pp since start of 2025
CompTIA: IT unemployment rose from 3.9% to 5.7%
Challenger: 78K tech jobs lost to AI in H1 2025
CAIS/Scale AI: AI fails 97.5% of real-world remote work projects at professional quality
Challenger: 54,694 AI-cited job cuts in 2025
BLS: SW dev growth revised down from 25% to 17%
Dallas Fed: young worker share fell 0.9pp
BLS: Computer programmer roles projected to decline further 6% (2024-2034)
Cognizant: computer/math roles 67% task exposure — second highest of all categories
Anthropic: Computer/math tasks ~33% of Claude.ai conversations, ~50% of API traffic
Indeed: Tech postings 34% below pre-pandemic; AI tech postings 45% above
CIO/Survey: 37% of tech professionals restructured due to gen AI in past 2 years
CIO/Robert Half: 61% of IT leaders plan to increase permanent headcount in H1 2026
METR (updated): AI made experienced open-source devs 18% slower (CI: -38% to +9%)
Goldman (Mei): AI impact 'largely confined to tech sector'; expects it to grow materially
Dallas Fed: Computer systems design employment down 5% since ChatGPT release
Wang et al.: AI development heavily concentrated on CS/Math — 7.6% of employment
Ramp CPO: 500+ features shipped in 2025 with only 25 PMs
Cooper/ACS: 7.2% unemployment for early-career CS grads vs 4.5% overall (2024)
Citadel/Indeed: SW engineer job postings up 11% YoY (2026)
Challenger: Tech layoffs up 51% YoY in Jan–Feb 2026; AI cited as primary driver
Anthropic: Programmers highest AI exposure (74.5%) but no unemployment impact detected

sources.title: The economic potential of generative AI
sources.excerpt: Generative AI could enable automation of up to 70% of business activities across occupations.

sources.title: Future of Jobs Report 2023
sources.excerpt: Employers estimate that 44% of workers' core skills will be disrupted in the next five years.

sources.title: The Simple Macroeconomics of AI
sources.excerpt: AI will increase productivity but impact on jobs more modest than often claimed; estimates ~5% of tasks fully automatable.

sources.title: The Potentially Large Effects of AI on Economic Growth
sources.excerpt: AI could expose 300 million full-time jobs globally to automation.

sources.title: New data show no AI jobs apocalypse — for now
sources.excerpt: Current employment data do not yet show large-scale AI-driven displacement.

sources.title: Beyond AI Exposure: Which Tasks are Cost-Effective to Automate with Computer Vision?
sources.excerpt: Only 23% of worker wages for vision tasks would be cost-effective to automate with AI today.

sources.title: AI And Automation Will Take 6% Of US Jobs By 2030
OECD Employment Outlook 2025: AI and the Labour Market
Alphabet Inc. 2024 10-K — Workforce & AI Automation Disclosures
sources.excerpt: Alphabet disclosed ongoing workforce restructuring, noting AI tools have reduced need for certain engineering and support roles while increasing demand for AI/ML specialists.

sources.title: Meta Platforms 2024 10-K — AI Efficiency & Headcount
sources.excerpt: Meta's 'Year of Efficiency' resulted in ~21,000 job cuts across 2023. Company cited AI-driven productivity gains enabling smaller teams to maintain output.

sources.title: How AI is Disrupting the Tech Job Market: Data from 20M Job Postings
sources.excerpt: Job openings grew 80% for AI scientists and 70% for ML engineers, while backend engineer postings declined 14%, frontend 24%, and data engineers 20%+.

sources.title: 78,000 Tech Jobs Lost to AI in H1 2025
sources.excerpt: 78,000 tech sector job losses attributed to AI in the first half of 2025, with QA testing, junior development, and IT support roles most affected.

sources.title: AI impacts in BLS employment projections
sources.publisher: Bureau of Labor Statistics (The Economics Daily)
sources.excerpt: Software developer employment projected to grow 17.9% (2023-2033). Despite AI exposure, this occupation is unlikely to experience employment decline because robust software needs are expected to support continued demand.

sources.title: Canaries in the Coal Mine? Six Facts about the Recent Employment Effects of AI
sources.publisher: Stanford Digital Economy Lab (Brynjolfsson, Chandar, Chen)
sources.excerpt: 13% relative decline in employment for workers ages 22-25 in most AI-exposed occupations. Software developer employment for ages 22-25 down nearly 20% since ChatGPT's launch.

sources.title: Displacement or Complementarity? The Labor Market Impact of Generative AI
sources.excerpt: 17% decrease in job postings for occupations in top quartile of automation potential, alongside 22% increase in postings for augmentation-prone occupations.

sources.title: The Anthropic Economic Index: Economic Primitives
sources.excerpt: Computer/mathematical tasks account for ~33% of all Claude.ai conversations and ~50% of API traffic, indicating concentrated AI impact on tech-adjacent roles.

sources.title: Young workers' employment drops in occupations with high AI exposure
sources.excerpt: Employment share of young workers (20-24) in most AI-exposed occupations fell from 16.4% to 15.5% between November 2022 and September 2025.

sources.title: How Will AI Affect the Global Workforce?
sources.excerpt: Young tech workers (20-30) unemployment risen ~3pp since start of 2025; tech employment share fallen below pre-pandemic trend.

sources.title: AI's Impact on Job Growth
sources.excerpt: Tech/info/media employment stopped growing at end of 2022, coinciding with ChatGPT release; less than 10% of firms using AI regularly as of mid-2025.

sources.title: US Labor Market Update: Tech Jobs May Be Below Pre-Pandemic Levels
sources.excerpt: Software development postings 28% below pre-pandemic baseline (Feb 2024). Annualized over ~1.3 years since ChatGPT launch: ~22%/yr decline rate.

sources.title: Software Development Postings Remain in the Doldrums
sources.excerpt: Software developer job postings 33% below pre-pandemic baseline as of November 2024. Annualized over ~2 years since ChatGPT launch: ~16.5%/yr decline rate.

sources.title: The US Tech Hiring Freeze Continues
sources.excerpt: Overall tech postings 36% below pre-pandemic baseline as of July 2025. Software engineer postings down 49%. Annualized over ~2.7 years: ~13.5%/yr decline rate.

sources.title: BLS Occupational Employment Statistics: Computer Programmers — Observed Decline
sources.excerpt: Computer programmer employment (routine coding roles) fell ~27.5% in roughly two years following ChatGPT's release (~13.8%/year annualized) — one of the largest two-year drops in any occupation tracked by BLS. Software developer employment remained flat.

sources.title: BLS Employment Projections: Computer Programmers 2024–2034
sources.excerpt: BLS projects a further 6% decline in programmer roles through 2034. Computer programmer employment (routine coding roles) fell ~27.5% in roughly two years following ChatGPT's release — one of the largest two-year drops in any occupation tracked by BLS. Software developer employment remained flat.

sources.title: 2025 Year-End Challenger Report: AI-Related Job Cuts
sources.excerpt: 54,694 job cuts cited AI as a reason in 2025, concentrated in tech, finance, and customer service sectors.

sources.title: CompTIA Tech Jobs Report: IT Unemployment Trends
sources.excerpt: IT unemployment rate rose from 3.9% to 5.7% between late 2024 and mid-2025, the highest level since 2021, driven by AI-related restructuring.

sources.title: Uplift Update: Measuring AI's Impact on Open-Source Developer Productivity
sources.excerpt: Updated study: original developers now estimated at -18% speedup (CI: -38% to +9%). Newly-recruited developers estimated at -4% (CI: -15% to +9%). Wide confidence intervals span zero.

sources.title: The Effects of Generative AI on Computing Workers' Productivity
sources.publisher: Microsoft/Accenture (Cui, Demirer, Jaffe, Musolff, Peng, Salz)
sources.excerpt: 26% increase in completed pull requests, 13.55% increase in commits, 38.38% increase in builds — but build success rate fell by 5.53 percentage points.

sources.title: The Impact of AI on Developer Productivity: Evidence from GitHub Copilot
sources.excerpt: RCT: developers completed a coding task 55.8% faster with GitHub Copilot assistance.

sources.title: New Work, New World 2026: How AI is Reshaping Work
sources.excerpt: Education task exposure jumped from 11% to 49% — a 4.5x increase driven by multimodal AI, advanced reasoning, and agentic systems. Based on reassessment of 18,000 tasks across 1,000 O*NET occupations.

sources.title: AI and the US Labor Market: Current Impact and Outlook
sources.excerpt: AI's labor market impact still small — only 5-10K/month drag on net job growth. Only 2½% of jobs exposed to automation today. Baseline forecast: 6-7% of workers displaced (range 3-14%), lowering annual hiring by 1M jobs and raising unemployment ~½pp. US creates 30M gross new jobs/year; does not anticipate job apocalypse. Impact so far largely confined to tech sector but expects it to grow materially.

sources.title: Remote Labor Index: Measuring AI Automation of Remote Work
sources.publisher: Center for AI Safety / Scale AI (Mazeika et al.)
sources.excerpt: Failures predominantly stem from file errors (18%), incompleteness (36%), sub-professional quality (46%), and inconsistencies (15%). Best agent (Manus) achieved only 2.5% automation rate across 240 real freelance projects.

sources.title: January 2026 US Labor Market Update: Jobs Mentioning AI Are Growing Amid Broader Hiring Weakness
sources.excerpt: the number of tech postings that mentioned AI was about 45% higher than in February 2020, while total tech postings were 34% below pre-pandemic levels

sources.title: AI is simultaneously aiding and replacing workers, wage data suggest
sources.publisher: Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas (J. Scott Davis)
sources.excerpt: Employment in the computer systems design and related services sector has declined 5 percent.

sources.title: How Well Does Agent Development Reflect Real-World Work?
sources.publisher: Carnegie Mellon / Stanford (Wang, Neubig, Fried, Yang et al.)
sources.excerpt: software engineering abilities has the potential to accelerate other varieties of work. However, this concentrated focus significantly overrepresents a domain that accounts for only 7.6% of total employment.

sources.title: State of IT jobs: AI sparks rapidly changing market for skills
sources.excerpt: 37% of tech professionals say their role has been 'redefined or restructured due to gen AI in the past two years.' 61% of IT leaders say they plan to increase permanent headcount in the first half of 2026.

sources.title: BLS Monthly Labor Review: Occupational employment projections to 2033
sources.publisher: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (Monthly Labor Review)
sources.excerpt: BLS projects computer programmer employment to decline 9.6% from 2023-2033 (~1% per year). Software developers projected to grow 17.9% over the same period.

sources.title: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook: Computer Programmers (2023-2033)
sources.excerpt: Overall employment of computer programmers is projected to decline 9.6 percent from 2023 to 2033.

sources.title: Your New Job Is to Onboard AI Agents: How AI Native Companies Actually Operate
sources.excerpt: In 2025, Ramp shipped 500+ features, reached $1B revenue, and did it all with 25 PMs.

sources.title: The 2026 Global Intelligence Crisis
sources.excerpt: In spite of the current displacement narrative – job postings for software engineers are rising rapidly, up 11% YoY.

sources.title: Should you still major in computer science?
sources.excerpt: According to the American Community Survey, 7.2 percent of early-career college graduates who majored in CS were unemployed in 2024—well above the 4.5 percent rate for all early-career graduates.

sources.title: Labor market impacts of AI: A new measure and early evidence
sources.excerpt: Computer Programmers are at the top, with 75% coverage, followed by Customer Service Representatives

sources.title: Challenger Report: February Cuts Plunge, YTD Hiring Falls 56%
sources.excerpt: Technology announced 11,039 job cuts in February for a total of 33,330 in 2026. That is an increase of 51% from the 22,042 cuts in this sector announced in the same period last year.

sources.title: The Impact of Large Language Models on Open-source Innovation: Evidence from GitHub Copilot
sources.excerpt: They found a 37-55 percent increase in commits, mainly through contributions building on others' work like debugging or small edits.

sources.title: AI and Productivity: Evidence from CodeFuse
sources.publisher: BIS Working Paper (Gambacorta, Qiu, Shan, Rees)
sources.excerpt: They show that 20 percent of the 55 percent total increase was directly attributable to AI-generated lines, suggesting genuine productivity effects beyond simple code generation.

sources.title: How much does AI impact development speed? An enterprise-based randomized controlled trial
sources.excerpt: They found a 21 percent reduction in time spent per task. Interestingly, more experienced developers saw bigger effects, possibly because AI requires substantial verification and judgment rather than simply accepting generated code.
